task_completion.description: Import the device_logs.json file into Power BI
task_completion.visual_evidence: Power BI Desktop or Power Query Editor is open, showing data loaded from 'device_logs.json'.

task_completion.description: Flatten the nested JSON data into required columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: Power Query Editor or Data View shows a flattened table containing at least the columns 'device_id', 'timestamp', and 'temperature'. The nested records/lists must be expanded.

task_completion.description: Set correct data types for timestamp and temperature
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Power Query Editor or Data View, the 'timestamp' column has a Date/Time data type icon (calendar/clock) and the 'temperature' column has a Decimal Number data type icon (1.2).

task_completion.description: Create a Line Chart visual on the report canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Line Chart visual is present on the main Power BI report canvas.

task_completion.description: Configure the Line Chart with timestamp, average temperature, and device_id
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Line Chart's field wells (or the chart itself) show 'timestamp' on the X-axis, 'temperature' on the Y-axis (specifically aggregated as Average), and 'device_id' in the Legend. The chart should display multiple lines representing different devices.

task_completion.description: Save the report as Smart_Home_Report.pbix on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI application title bar reads 'Smart_Home_Report - Power BI Desktop' or a file explorer/save dialog shows 'Smart_Home_Report.pbix' saved to the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Power BI Desktop's interface (Power Query Editor, report canvas, field panes) rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to generate the .pbix file.

integrity.description: Verify the chart uses the actual synthetic dataset
integrity.visual_evidence: The Line Chart displays realistic data matching the dataset properties: temperatures ranging between approximately 18-24°C and multiple distinct device IDs (e.g., D11, D12, etc.), rather than manually entered dummy data.

integrity.description: Verify the line chart is a genuine Power BI visual
integrity.visual_evidence: The line chart is a native Power BI visual with standard interactive elements (axes, legend, title, tooltips) and not an imported static image or drawn shapes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\PowerBITasks\device_logs.json. The nested JSON must be flattened to include 'device_id', 'timestamp', and 'temperature' columns. The visual must be a Line Chart with 'timestamp' on the X-axis, 'temperature' (aggregated as Average) on the Y-axis, and 'device_id' as the Legend. The output file must be saved exactly as C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Smart_Home_Report.pbix. The underlying dataset contains 360 readings from 15 devices (named like D11, D53) with temperatures ranging from 18-24°C.
